strategic_profile.executive_summary: The University of New Mexico is a flagship public research university and designated Hispanic-Serving Institution (HSI). It pairs a highly accessible, numbers-driven general admissions process with elite, R1-level research opportunities, particularly in engineering, medicine, and the sciences.

analyst_takeaways.insight: General admission is exceptionally accessible, operating on a transparent GPA and core-unit threshold rather than competitive holistic review.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students meeting the 2.5 GPA and 16-unit requirement are virtually guaranteed entry, making it an excellent safety or strategic financial target.

analyst_takeaways.insight: UNM holds Carnegie R1 status and maintains deep partnerships with Sandia and Los Alamos National Laboratories.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Undergraduates in STEM have uncommon access to world-class equipment, national lab internships, and high-level faculty research.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Top in-state applicants can compete for the Regents' Scholarship, which covers full tuition, fees, and housing.
analyst_takeaways.implication: High-achieving local students can secure an elite-level education with zero debt.

analyst_takeaways.insight: As a federally designated Hispanic-Serving Institution, nearly half the undergraduate body identifies as Hispanic or Latino.
analyst_takeaways.implication: The campus offers a richly diverse, inclusive environment reflective of the American Southwest.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Diverse, culturally rich, and welcoming. Features a large commuter and part-time population, though on-campus life is active. Some student concerns exist regarding safety and crime in the surrounding city area.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Located along historic Route 66 in central Albuquerque; heavily car-dependent with frequent student complaints regarding parking availability.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Extremely high. Over $243 million in annual research expenditures with cutting-edge facilities like the Center for High Technology Materials and the Comprehensive Cancer Center.

current_status.test_policy_details: Standardized test scores (SAT/ACT) are not required for general admission but are considered if submitted. They may be used for course placement or specific merit scholarships.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist data for general undergraduate admission is not publicly disclosed. The School of Medicine maintains a ranked Alternate List.

gpa.notes: Minimum required cumulative GPA for automatic consideration is 2.50.

testing.policy_note: ACT submission rate is 8%. Test-optional policy heavily utilized.

colleges.name: College of Arts & Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students interested in natural sciences, humanities, or those beginning as 'Undeclared'.
colleges.student_archetype: Broad academic explorers and pre-professional students.

majors.name: Psychology
majors.notes: One of the largest and most popular undergraduate degree concentrations at the university.

colleges.name: Anderson School of Management
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: For students aiming for corporate, accounting, or entrepreneurial careers in the Southwest.

majors.name: Business Administration
majors.notes: The most popular undergraduate degree at UNM, with 447 bachelor's degrees awarded in 2024.

colleges.name: College of Nursing
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Highly driven pre-health students with strong science backgrounds.
colleges.housing_profile: General university housing, close proximity to North Campus/Medical facilities.
colleges.student_archetype: Clinical care and public health focused.

majors.name: Nursing
majors.special_requirements: Requires holistic admissions review for clinical placement.
majors.notes: Highly competitive. Nursing is the third most popular major at UNM overall.

application_deadlines.notes: Fall term priority application date. Rolling admissions apply.

primary_factors: Completion of 16 specific college preparatory units
holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: General admissions is formulaic (minimum 2.5 GPA and core unit completion). Essays and portfolios are explicitly not required for general admission.

major_selection_tactics: Applying to University College as 'Undeclared' is highly normalized, with ~14% of freshmen starting without a declared major.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Students aiming for highly competitive clinical or engineering programs typically enter in 'pre-major' status and must clear specific departmental GPA and prerequisite thresholds to officially declare.

financials.tuition_model: Flat base rate with some differential tuition for specialized programs (e.g., Engineering, Nursing).

scholarships.name: Regents' Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Covers base tuition, fees, and housing. Renewable for 4 years.

scholarships.name: Amigo Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Waives out-of-state tuition, granting in-state rates to non-residents.

scholarships.name: Transfer Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Renewable for 2 years for transfer students.

ap_policy.general_rule: Grants credit for scores of 3 or higher on most AP exams.
ap_policy.usage: Credits can be applied toward General Education core requirements or major prerequisites.

ib_policy.general_rule: Grants credit for Higher Level and Standard Level exams.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: A minimum of 15 credit hours must be earned in residence at UNM to receive a degree.

what_it_takes: Completion of 16 specific college preparatory units
Early application (by December 1) for priority merit scholarship consideration
red_flags: Failing to complete required high school core units
Missing the December 1st scholarship priority deadline
insights: UNM provides top-tier research access for undergrads due to close ties with Sandia and Los Alamos National Labs.
In-state students view UNM as a massive financial bargain, particularly with the Lottery and Regents scholarships.
Students occasionally express safety concerns regarding the surrounding neighborhoods in Albuquerque.
Parking constraints and administrative bureaucracy are frequent points of frustration among the student body.
